Please always cite any links you referenced from the above search results in your response in markdown format.

Website URL: https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration
Website Title: BIQc — Business Intelligence Centre
Website Content:

</external_links>
<user_query>
review this card in CMO report of calibration on https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration  review all edge functions that support customer review inrtelligence - the content is incorrect - this section should reveal all customer reviews in last 12 months across all Google reviews, forums, and anyb review platforms postive and negative with action plans to improve operaTIONS WIith scores if available - truth frpom source onkly a new SEm rush edge was created and there are many other edgen function capabilites to assist 
</user_query>

I’ll audit the calibration CMO “customer review intelligence” card and trace every supporting edge function, then I’ll identify exactly why the content is wrong and implement a source-truth fix so it includes last-12-month reviews (positive/negative), scores, and operations action plans. I’m starting by mapping the relevant frontend card and Supabase edge functions now.

I’ve located the calibration and edge-function surface area. Next I’m narrowing to the exact CMO report card component and the server functions feeding its “customer review intelligence” content so I can pinpoint where incorrect data is injected.

I found the exact card in `ChiefMarketingSummary` and it currently only relies on a narrow review payload (mostly Google/Glassdoor plus lightweight aggregation). I’m now reading the full data pipeline from calibration state and edge functions to identify all missing sources and where to enforce a strict last-12-month, source-only output.

I’ve confirmed the backend orchestration path: `backend/routes/calibration.py` merges review data from multiple edge functions, including `browse-ai-reviews` and the new `semrush-domain-intel`. I’m now auditing the exact merge logic to see why the card still misses full 12‑month cross-platform truth and actionability.

I’ve isolated the root issue: the card is currently driven by partial, mixed-signal review data (Google/Glassdoor-centric) and not a strict, source-verified 12‑month customer-review dataset across platforms/forums.

I’m going to make three concrete changes now:
- Build a canonical `review_intelligence` payload in `backend/routes/calibration.py` that:
  - normalizes reviews from Serper + `browse-ai-reviews`,
  - keeps source URLs/platform labels,
  - filters to last 12 months only when date evidence exists,
  - computes positive/negative splits and platform scores,
  - generates operations-focused action plans from negative themes.
- Pass that payload through calibration state (`useCalibrationState`) so the CMO card can consume it directly.
- Replace the current card rendering in `ChiefMarketingSummary` to show cross-platform 12‑month truth (including forums), source links, sentiment splits, scores, and action plans.

After edits I’ll run targeted checks to verify no syntax/runtime regressions.

I’ve implemented the backend canonical `review_intelligence` pipeline and wired it into the CMO card with a strict 12‑month source-truth path. I’m now running targeted static/runtime checks to catch any syntax or integration issues before I summarize what changed.

## Findings (and fixes applied) - **Critical**: the CMO customer-review card was **not enforcing a strict last-12-month window** and mixed inferred trust signals with review snippets, which can produce misleading output. - **High**: platform coverage was narrow in UI (mainly Google/Glassdoor), while forum/review-platform evidence wasn’t surfaced as a unified customer-review truth set. - **High**: operational actions were not tightly tied to negative customer-review evidence. - **Medium**: edge orchestration metadata did not expose `browse-ai-reviews` and `semrush-domain-intel` status alongside other tools. I implemented a full fix across backend aggregation + frontend rendering for the calibration CMO card used on [Calibration](https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration). ## What changed - Built a canonical `review_intelligence` payload in `backend/routes/calibration.py`: - merges Serper review results + forum results + `browse-ai-reviews`, - parses source dates, keeps only date-verifiable reviews in last 12 months, - keeps source URLs/platform/sentiment/rating where available, - computes cross-platform scores and totals, - generates evidence-backed operational action plans from negative reviews. - Expanded review-source discovery in enrichment scan: - added forum review query path and merged into enrichment input. - Wired canonical payload into frontend state: - `frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js` now carries `review_intelligence`. - Upgraded the card UI in `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`: - new strict “source-verified · 12m window” mode, - all dated customer reviews (positive/negative/neutral) across platforms/forums with source links, - platform score cards (with scope labels), - operational action plan block based on negative evidence, - explicit handling of undated signals (excluded from 12-month totals). ```888:898:backend/routes/calibration.py def _build_review_intelligence( google_review_search: Dict[str, Any], review_search: Dict[str, Any], forum_review_search: Dict[str, Any], browse_ai_reviews: Dict[str, Any], ) -> Dict[str, Any]: now_utc = datetime.now(timezone.utc) window_start = now_utc - timedelta(days=365) ``` ```1957:1976:backend/routes/calibration.py review_intelligence = _build_review_intelligence( google_review_search, review_search, forum_review_search, browse_ai_reviews if isinstance(browse_ai_reviews, dict) else {}, ) if review_intelligence: enrichment["review_intelligence"] = review_intelligence totals = review_intelligence.get("totals") or {} enrichment.setdefault("review_aggregation", {}).update({ "combined_score": review_intelligence.get("overall_score"), "positive_count": totals.get("positive_last_12_months", 0), "negative_count": totals.get("negative_last_12_months", 0), ``` ```979:988:frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js {/* ── SECTION 5A: CUSTOMER REVIEW INTELLIGENCE ── */}

Customer Review Intelligence

{strictHasReviewBlock ? 'source-verified · 12m window' : `confidence: ${customerReviews.confidenceBand}`} ``` ## Edge functions reviewed in this path - `browse-ai-reviews` (direct customer/staff review extraction) - `semrush-domain-intel` (score enrichment context, now explicitly surfaced in edge metadata) - `deep-web-recon` (supporting market/sentiment signals) - `social-enrichment` (trust/social context) - Orchestration in `backend/routes/calibration.py` (`/calibration/enrichment/website`) ## Validation run - `python -m py_compile backend/routes/calibration.py` ✅ - Direct Python smoke test of `_build_review_intelligence(...)` with mixed Google/Trustpilot/Reddit/Whirlpool sample data ✅ (returned expected counts, sentiment split, action plan, platform scores) - `npx prettier --check` on edited frontend files: parse OK, formatting warnings only. - `npx eslint` couldn’t run in this repo context due ESLint v9 flat-config mismatch (no `eslint.config.*` detected). ## Residual risk / gap - Some platforms do not expose per-review dates in snippets/API responses; those entries are now explicitly excluded from 12-month totals (truth-preserving), but this can reduce visible volume until richer dated sources are connected.
